Operation 'deleteWebhook' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. The current summary is generic and the description is completely empty. The improved summary specifies the deletion is by ID. The new description clarifies: (1) what the operation does (removes webhook registration), (2) the input requirement (webhook ID), (3) authentication context, (4) the side effect (webhook stops receiving notifications), (5) the return value (success confirmation), and (6) the permanent nature of the action. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what consequences it has.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a registered webhook by ID
data.description_suggestion: Removes a webhook registration from the system by its unique identifier. The webhook will no longer receive event notifications after deletion. Requires authentication and the webhook ID must belong to the authenticated user's account. Returns a success confirmation. This operation is permanent and cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: The current summary is generic and the description is completely empty. The improved summary specifies the deletion is by ID. The new description clarifies: (1) what the operation does (removes webhook registration), (2) the input requirement (webhook ID), (3) authentication context, (4) the side effect (webhook stops receiving notifications), (5) the return value (success confirmation), and (6) the permanent nature of the action. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what consequences it has.

data.current_description: Update transaction metadata such as category.
